highlights.title: Read text and Markdown too
highlights.description: Got a snippet of text or a .txt / .md file? You can now paste it in or drop the file. Yomitomo detects the encoding, suggests a title, and shows Markdown front matter up top — text lands in your Library just like an ebook.

highlights.title: Drag to fix your selection
highlights.description: Selected a little too much or too little? Handles now appear at both ends of a selection, so you can drag to extend or shrink it instead of starting over. Works in web articles, ebooks, and PDFs.

highlights.title: Clear on whether your books get uploaded
highlights.description: Worried that an imported private PDF or ebook leaves your device? The import dialog now states plainly that files stay local; only when you use a configured AI model is the relevant content sent to the endpoint you set.

highlights.title: Smoother ebook and PDF paging
highlights.description: Ebooks add tap-to-page hot zones, an adaptive spread layout, and smoother table-of-contents jumps; PDFs now open at a zoom that fits your window width and leave room for the annotation rail.

highlights.title: No more stray selections or squished covers
highlights.description: Page-turn taps no longer trigger a paragraph selection, and wider covers keep their proportions after a refresh instead of getting squeezed.
